title: Housekeeping Assistant - Gormanston Wood Nursing Home
description: **We are looking for a Housekeeping Assistant to work in our Gormanston Wood Nursing Home (Co. Meath) as part of Trinity Care Group a well\-respected, multi\-award\-winning Nursing Home Group, part of the international DomusVi Group.** **We provide private residential, convalescent and rehabilitative care in our purpose\-built Nursing Homes. We have centres in Dublin, Meath, Louth, Kildare, Kilkenny, and Cavan.** **Key Responsibilities:** **To ensure the highest standard of cleanliness in all areas at all times. Responsible for carrying out of your duties in the laundry including bed linens etc., and personal items for clients.** **Key Responsibilities:** * To be responsible for the thorough servicing of your allocated area to the defined standard. * To offer excellent service to all clients/visitors and ensure that all queries, complaints and compliments are relayed back to your supervisor. * To maintain the appearance of your trolley, press and working area at all times, restocking your trolley before going off duty, and ensuring presses are secure. * To have an understanding of usage, safety and hazards of all chemicals and cleaning products. * Record keeping. * Ensure that you maintain the cleanliness and good order of your area of assignment and associated areas to the highest possible standard. * Ensure efficient and economical use of materials and equipment. * To adhere to specifications, standards and procedures while at work. * To report mechanical defects and needs for repairs. * To take all necessary steps to ensure the maximum security of your area of assignment and all equipment and supplies contained therein. * To carry our any reasonable requests by management **Skills:** Cleaning, Chemicals, Domestic Cleaning

title: IT & Continuous Improvement Assistant
description: **Reports to:** Continuous Improvement Lead **Job Purpose:** Support the day\-to\-day IT needs of the factory while assisting with continuous improvement, digitalisation, training and process standardisation projects **Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to:** **Basic IT Tasks:** * Provide first\-line support for IT\-related issues within the factory environment * Troubleshoot hardware, software, printer, scanner, mobile device and basis network issues * Escalate complex issues to Group IT or external providers where required * Support users with Microsoft 365, shared drives, factory systems and standard business applications * Assist with routine IT checks, updates and maintenance activities in line with company procedures and Group IT guidance * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems **Continuous Improvement \& Digitalisation Support:** * Support continuous improvement projects across production, operations, quality, engineering and training * Assist with process mapping, data collection, problem solving and action tracking * Help identify opportunities to reduce manual administration, improve information flow and standardise ways of working * Support the development of simple digital tools, forms, trackers, reports and dashboards * Assist with the rollout of factory improvement projects, ensuring users are trained and supported **Operational Training:** * Assist in operational training sessions for new starters, providing guidance and support as needed to facilitate smooth onboarding processes **IT Asset Management:** * Maintain an accurate inventory of all IT assets, including hardware, software licenses, and peripherals * Track the procurement, deployment, and retirement of IT assets according to established procedures and budgetary constraints * Conduct periodic audits to verify the physical location and condition of IT assets, reconciling discrepancies as necessary * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems * Professional development is encouraged to support career growth and progression **Qualifications/ Skills/ Experience:** **Essential:** * Demonstrated proficiency in basic IT tasks including hardware and software troubleshooting, and system maintenance. Ideally 1 year experience in IT environment * Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple responsibilities simultaneously * Show initiative and self\-motivation * Demonstrate a strong aptitude for problem\-solving * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively convey technical information and collaborate with cross\-functional teams * Proven ability to identify, analyse, and resolve IT\-related issues in a timely and efficient manner, minimising disruption to operations **Desirable:** * Experience in a manufacturing, food production or regulated environment * Familiarity with continuous improvement tools such as 5S, Lean, root cause analysis, standard work or process mapping * Experience with SharePoint, Power Apps, Power BI, Excel reporting or similar digital tools * Experience creating training materials, SOP’s videos or presentations * Knowledge of IT asset management, inventory control or software licence tracking * Understanding of basic IT security practices * Experience supporting system rollouts, user training or change management * Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) may be considered, based on relevant experience, training, or voluntary work Pay: €35,500\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience providing first\-line IT support to end users? * What Microsoft applications and systems have you used regularly? * Have you been involved in any process improvement or continuous improvement projects? * Are you comfortable providing hands\-on support on a factory floor, including troubleshooting PCs, printers, scanners, tablets, and user issues? Work Location: In person

title: IT Co-Ordinator
description: Glen Dimplex is a privately owned, international group headquartered in Ireland which operates across four main product areas: Heating \& Ventilation, Consumer Appliances, Flame and Precision Cooling. Glen Dimplex is one of the world's leading manufacturers of electric heating appliances and is a global leader in the development and delivery of low carbon solutions for space and water heating, cooling, and ventilation. Our purpose is to be a leader in the transition to a more sustainable world. This is underpinned by our ‘Empower Now’ Sustainability strategy. On our sustainability journey, we will listen to our stakeholders, learn about new processes and technologies, partner with experts that will challenge us, empower our staff to go further, and set long\-term goals that are embedded into our business plans. Details of brands and our can be found on our website. **ROLE OVERVIEW \- IT COORDINATOR** Glen Dimplex is seeking a highly organised and proactive IT Co\-ordinator to join our technology team. Reporting directly to the CTO, this role serves as a crucial operational support function within the IT department, ensuring smooth day\-to\-day operations and enabling the CTO and wider IT leadership team to focus on strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate will be an exceptional communicator with strong organisational skills, have the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment, with a keen interest in leveraging Office365 and modern Microsoft technologies including Power BI, Copilot, and Viva Engage to drive efficiency and innovation. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** **Adoption Platform** **IT Digital content platform for end\-user training and adoption** * Take ownership of the platform and become the subject\-matter expert, creating content and enabling others in IT to do the same * Support Community of Practice owners in developing and updating content * Organise the structure and approach for how we share and develop content * Own the product roadmap, working with the supplier to ensure required features are incorporated **Supplier \& Contract Management** * Set up new IT suppliers, including onboarding, account setup, and establishing working relationships * Organise and manage IT supplier contracts, ensuring compliance with company policies and renewal timelines * Maintain comprehensive records of all vendor agreements, SLAs, and contract terms * Raise and process purchase orders for IT equipment, software licenses, and services * Liaise with suppliers to resolve issues, negotiate terms, and ensure service delivery meets expectations * Track IT expenditure and maintain budget documentation **Meeting \& Workshop Facilitation** * Organize and facilitate IT leadership meetings and workshops * Coordinate logistics including room bookings, technology setup, catering, and materials * Prepare meeting agendas, distribute materials in advance, and capture accurate minutes * Track action items and follow up on deliverables to ensure accountability * Schedule and coordinate cross\-functional meetings with other departments **Business Communications \& Stakeholder Management** * Develop communication plans for providing notifications and updates to the business regarding technology initiatives * Communicate IT updates, system changes, and planned maintenance to business customers across the organization * Develop clear, user\-friendly communications that translate technical information for non\-technical audiences * Manage communication channels, including email, Viva Engage, Teams, and collaboration platforms, to ensure timely information dissemination **Presentation \& Documentation** * Create professional presentations for internal stakeholders, executive leadership, and external audiences (PowerPoint, Copilot, and draw.io) * Develop clear and compelling visual materials that communicate technical concepts effectively * Maintain and update IT documentation, policies, and procedural guidelines * Produce reports and dashboards on IT metrics, project status, and departmental performance **Executive \& Administrative Support** * Provide dedicated support to the CTO, including diary management, meeting scheduling, and priority management * Coordinate internal and external appointments, ensuring optimal use of the CTO’s time * Prepare briefing materials and meeting agendas * Handle correspondence and communications on behalf of the CTO when appropriate **Technology \& Innovation** * Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards to provide insights on IT operations, budgets, and project status * Create and deploy Power Apps solutions to streamline departmental workflows and processes * Leverage Microsoft Copilot to enhance productivity in documentation, communication, and data analysis * Stay current with emerging Microsoft 365 tools and identify opportunities to apply them within the IT department * Champion the adoption of low\-code/no\-code solutions to automate routine tasks **REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS \& SKILLS** **Essential:** * Proven experience in an administrative, coordination, or support role, preferably within an IT or technical environment * Exceptional organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities simultaneously * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and collaboration tools * Keen interest and enthusiasm for learning and working with Microsoft 365, including Power BI, Power Apps, Microsoft Copilot, and Viva Engage * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to tailor messages for different audiences * High attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to handle confidential information with discretion and professionalism * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build relationships across all levels of the organization * Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and take initiative * Passion for technology and continuous learning **Desirable:** * Hands\-on experience with Power BI, Power Apps, Viva Engage, or other Microsoft Power Platform and Microsoft 365 tools * Understanding of IT terminology and systems * Experience with contract management or procurement processes * Experience supporting C\-level executives and directors * Experience with data visualization and reporting tools * Background in internal communications or stakeholder engagement * Experience developing communication strategies or plans * Familiarity with enterprise social networking and employee engagement platforms **PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES** * Proactive and solutions\-oriented mindset * Professional demeanour with strong business acumen * Adaptable and comfortable working in a dynamic environment * Strong problem\-solving abilities * Team player with a collaborative approach * Commitment to continuous improvement and learning * Curious and eager to explore new technologies and tools * Customer\-focused with strong service orientation **OUR VALUES AND CULTURE AND BENEFITS:** The Glen Dimplex values are important guiding principles and define the way all employees across the Group work: We Think Customer, We Care About People, We Value Innovation and We Keep It Simple. Our core values are ingrained in our DNA and play an active part in everything we do. Each one reminds us to stay true to ourselves whilst driving us to create innovative products and solutions for our customers. At Glen Dimplex, we are strongly committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all. We are focused on creating and inclusive culture and believe that it is essential to creating a dynamic and supportive workplace. We are committed to fostering a culture that embraces and celebrates differences in race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, and ability. We believe diverse perspectives and experiences are essential to our success as a company and a team. We believe in investing in our employees' well\-being and recognise the importance of work\-life balance. We offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees' physical, mental, and financial health. Our benefits package includes: * Competitive salary * Generous annual leave allowance * Private Health Insurance * Pension and Death in Service Benefit * Employee Assistance Programme * Permanent Health Insurance * Life Assurance * Supportive Family Leave policies. **We also offer additional benefits such as** * \-Generous discounts on Company products. * \-Bike to Work / Tax Saver initiatives. * \-Regular wellbeing talks supports and resources**.** By submitting your application, you agree that Glen Dimplex may collect your personal data for recruiting and related purposes. Glen Dimplex Privacy Policy explains what personal information we may process, where we may process your personal information, our purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Glen Dimplex's use of your personal information.

title: IT Support - SharePoint
description: **IT Support \- SharePoint** **Permanent \| North Dublin, Meath, Louth, Cavan, Monaghan \| €50,000 \- €55,000 \+ Benefits** TechHeads are recruiting an experienced **IT Support \- SharePoint** professional to join a growing Group IT Applications team supporting users and business functions across a global organisation. This role offers the opportunity to take ownership of a large\-scale SharePoint and Microsoft 365 environment, supporting governance, compliance, collaboration and continuous improvement initiatives across multiple departments. **What You'll Be Doing** * Administering and optimising SharePoint Online and Microsoft 365 * Managing governance, retention and compliance policies * Supporting Microsoft Teams, Entra ID and Microsoft Purview * Monitoring tenant health, storage and usage trends * Gathering requirements from business stakeholders * Providing user support, guidance and training * Acting as an escalation point for complex technical issues **What We're Looking For** * 3\+ years' SharePoint Online administration experience * Strong Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Teams knowledge * Experience with Data Governance and Retention Policies * Knowledge of Entra ID, Purview and SharePoint permissions * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills * Understanding of ITIL principles **Eligibility:** Candidates must hold an EU passport or a valid Stamp 4 Visa.

title: IT Systems Administrator
description: **IT Systems Administrator** * Dublin 2, located close to Grand Canal dock station * Excellent salary \& benefits package; pension, healthcare \& bonus * Hybrid working arrangements A well\-established professional services organisation is seeking an experienced IT Systems Administrator to join its growing internal technology team. This is an excellent opportunity to work across modern infrastructure, cloud technologies, networking, and security within a collaborative and fast\-paced environment. **Responsibilities:** * Managing and supporting Windows Server and Microsoft 365 environments * Administering Active Directory, Azure AD, DNS, DHCP, and Group Policy * Supporting Exchange Online, Intune, SharePoint, Teams, and related services * Monitoring infrastructure performance and resolving technical issues proactively * Supporting networking technologies including Cisco and Meraki environments * Assisting with cybersecurity, patching, firewalls, VPNs, and compliance initiatives * Acting as an escalation point for technical support queries * Working closely with third\-party vendors and internal stakeholders **Requirements:** * Strong experience in Systems Administration within enterprise environments * Expertise across Windows Server, Microsoft 365, and virtualised infrastructure * Solid networking knowledge including switches, routers, VPNs, and wireless solutions * Experience with security tooling and infrastructure best practices * Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to manage competing priorities * Experience with SQL Server and enterprise applications is beneficial * Exposure to professional services environments is an advantage Apply below or reach out with any questions!

title: Inbound Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** ‘ARE YOU 'CUSTOMER FOCUSED \& TEAM PLAYER' WE LIKE THAT ABOUT YOU Sandvik is a global, high\-tech engineering group, providing front\-line products and services for the mining and construction industries. The Sandvik Group has been in operation since 1862 and today employs some 40,000 people, working in approximately 160 countries. Our diverse solutions delivered by our highly qualified and proficient employees enhance our customers’ productivity, safety and their profitability. **We are now recruiting for two Inbound** **Specialists \- for** **permanent roles in our Dublin Office.** **JOB PURPOSE** You manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio and ensure agreed performance targets are met, including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy. You proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions. You also conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required. You ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. End to end responsibility including PO placement, PO tracking from order to receipt, expediting urgent orders, resolving invoice discrepancies, data integrity maintenance and responding to sales region queries. You are also responsible for system parameter maintenance including lead\-time, safety stock levels and classification. You improve availability and reduce lead\-time through accurate and timely forecasting. **MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES** **Key performance areas** * Day to day management of operational buying activities and driving supplier performance for the respective supplier base with focus on parts availability and working capital. * Expectations focus on making and implementing sound business decisions based on available data. * High level of collaboration with internal stakeholders including: Sourcing, Engineering and Customer Service. **Key Activities** * Manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio * Ensure agreed performance targets are met including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy * Proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions * Conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required * Ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. End to end responsibility including PO placement, PO tracking from order to receipt, expediting urgent orders, resolving invoice discrepancies, data integrity maintenance and responding to sales region queries. * Responsible for system parameter maintenance including lead\-time, safety stock levels and classification. Improve availability and reduce lead\-time through accurate and timely forecasting. * Design and implement inventory reduction initiatives such as Vendor Managed Inventory * Support supply chain improvement projects and work closely with other departments as needed. * Understand and improve customer experience by driving improvements in the order fulfilment rate. * Ensure that the supplier portfolio is optimized: eliminate dormant suppliers, rationalize the active supply base * Other ad hoc duties as required by management **Experience** You have extensive experience in a related supply chain role and have strong knowledge of Logistics Systems and Supply Chain Management. You will also have a deep understanding of purchasing, planning and inventory control. Strong experience of supplier management and/or inventory planning is a must, as is strong knowledge of ERP systems and business management tools. You speak fluent English (another European language would be of benefit) and have the ability to travel within the region. General knowledge of Aftermarket business is an advantage. **Education, Licenses \& Certificates** * A degree level or equivalent, ideally in a business or supply chain discipline. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location**: Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to**: Purchasing Manager **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply**? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is 12th June 2026\.** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU**

title: Inbound Transport Coordinator
description: **About Keelings** Keelings is a 100% Irish\-owned family business passionate about fresh produce. Operating across Ireland, the UK, and Europe, we grow, source, and supply top\-quality fruit and vegetables. Our success is built on innovation, sustainability, and strong partnerships \- powered by our dedicated people. **Purpose of the Role** Responsible for the day\-to\-day coordination and execution of inbound transport operations across multiple international supply routes and transport modes. Operating within a fast\-paced environment, the role supports the efficient, compliant and cost\-effective movement of products from global suppliers through to the business distribution network. The successful candidate will work closely with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents, shipping partners and internal departments to ensure inbound deliveries are planned, tracked and managed effectively. The role requires strong organisational skills, attention to detail and the ability to respond quickly to operational issues in order to maintain continuity of supply and service standards. This position plays an important role in supporting inbound transport operations, monitoring logistics performance, maintaining accurate records and ensuring compliance with customs, legal and company requirements across Ireland, the UK, EU and global import markets. The role will contribute to a culture of teamwork, accountability and continuous improvement while building strong working relationships with logistics partners and internal stakeholders. **Main Responsibilities** **Inbound Logistics Coordination** * Coordinate daily inbound logistics activities across road, sea and air freight operations. * Support the planning and scheduling of inbound deliveries in line with operational requirements and product shelf\-life considerations. * Monitor inbound shipments and proactively communicate delays, disruptions or service issues to relevant stakeholders. * Liaise with suppliers, hauliers, shipping lines and freight forwarders to ensure timely collection and delivery of products. * Assist with container scheduling, haulage planning and transport coordination to support daily operational targets. * Track supplier delivery performance and escalate issues where necessary. * Support customs clearance activities by coordinating documentation with customs agents and logistics partners. * Ensure all freight documentation, transport records and logistics systems are accurately updated within required timelines. * Assist with coordinating global imports by air and sea, ensuring all required documentation is completed correctly. * Maintain strong working relationships with third\-party logistics providers and transport partners. * Support the monitoring of inbound transport costs and assist with cost control initiatives. **Operational Support** * Work closely with the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director and wider logistics team to support smooth day\-to\-day operations. * Collaborate with Procurement, Sales, Warehouse, Technical and Planning teams to ensure efficient supply chain coordination. * Act as a key point of contact for inbound transport queries and operational updates. * Support logistics planning activities during peak trading periods and seasonal demand fluctuations. * Assist in identifying opportunities for process improvements and operational efficiencies. * Ensure all logistics activities are carried out in accordance with company Health \& Safety standards and operational procedures. * Support onboarding and training activities for new team members where required. **Compliance \& Administration** * Ensure compliance with customs procedures, transport regulations and approved haulier policies. * Assist in monitoring customs entry accuracy and resolving customs\-related queries. * Maintain accurate operational records and support KPI reporting for inbound logistics activities. * Complete daily and weekly administration tasks including shipment tracking, reporting and system updates. * Assist with invoice queries, transport cost reconciliations and supplier documentation checks. * Support supplier performance monitoring through KPI tracking and service reviews. * Ensure all required compliance and training activities are completed within designated timelines. **Key Accountabilities** * Coordination of inbound deliveries to ensure products arrive on time and in full. * Accurate maintenance of transport records, shipment documentation and logistics systems. * Effective communication with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents and internal stakeholders. * Support delivery of inbound transport service levels and operational targets. * Timely escalation and resolution of inbound logistics issues. * Maintenance of customs compliance and transport administration standards. * Contribution to continuous improvement initiatives within the logistics function. * Delivery of accurate reporting and operational support to the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director. **Skills \& Experience** * Previous experience in logistics, transport coordination or supply chain administration, ideally within fresh produce, FMCG or a fast\-paced environment. * Understanding of international freight operations across road, sea and air freight. * Working knowledge of customs procedures and import/export documentation would be advantageous. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. * Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving ability. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced and constantly changing environment. * Good commercial awareness and understanding of logistics operations. * Strong IT and systems skills, including Microsoft Office and transport/logistics systems. * Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team. **Special Circumstances** * Flexibility may be required to work outside normal business hours in line with operational requirements. * Participation in an on\-call rota required to support operational issues. * Role operates on a 5 days over 7 working pattern. * Occasional travel may be required depending on operational needs. **Education \& Qualifications** * Degree, diploma or relevant experience in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business or a related discipline. * Experience in transport coordination, logistics administration or supply chain operations. * Knowledge of customs clearance processes and Incoterms would be advantageous. * Understanding of inbound logistics and international freight operations preferred. **Benefits:** * Staff discount at Keelings Farm Shop and subsidized canteen * Free On\-site parking with access to electric chargers * Wellness Initiatives such as wellness week **Keelings Values** * People Matter * Teamwork * Integrity * Passion for Achievement **Inclusion \& Diversity Statement** **In Keelings we value all perspectives and knowledge that our colleagues bring to our business. We are committed to forming a diverse and inclusive team where all can share their unique experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all cultures and backgrounds and are dedicated to forming an environment of inclusiveness and diversity where all can thoroughly feel a sense of belonging.** **Reasonable Accommodation Statement** **If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please let us know at** recruitment\_keelings@keelings.com **Recruitment Process** * 1\. CV Submission \& email acknowledgment of receipt * 2\. Phone Screening * 3\. 1st Interview * 4\. 2nd Interview * 5\. Outcome \& Feedback *Keelings Careers \-* *https://www.keelings.com/careers\-keelings/* *Keelings Strategy \& Values \-* *https://www.keelings.com/our\-people/*

title: Information Technology Cross-Function Assistant Vice President
description: 4 Jun 2026 Dublin, D, IE, D02 KF20 **Who we are** -------------- At SMBC Group EMEA, we are united by a shared purpose: to be a trusted long‑term partner to our clients. You will lead the design, development, architectural integration and evolution, maintenance and delivery of e\-invoicing platform across SMBC EMEA entities. This is a unique opportunity to partner with technical delivery team to implement the regulatory compliance solution, service ownership and operational excellence of integrating invoice solutions through a strategic architecture, driving transformation and influencing how SMBC supports Operations, Finance and Tax functions. The role requires strong technical expertise, and service ownership to mature this capability for EMEA region, ensuring platform stability, continuous improvement and standardisation to enhance data quality, controls and alignment with global best practices. The role also encompasses system development, major enhancements, and strategic upgrades.**Position Description** ------------------------ * Product Strategy \& Roadmap: Define and implement strategy, roadmap planning, and architectural direction for rolling out e\-invoice services across SMBC EMEA entities. * Backlog Management: Own, and refine the product backlog, prioritise technical and customer facing requirements. * Service Ownership: Owning the end‑to‑end delivery lifecycle covering solution design, development oversight, system testing, and controlled implementation of e\-invoicing platform. * System Integration: Enable the integration of e\-invoicing platform – PAGERO, with multiple invoice processing systems, including COUPA, Accounts Payable (ERP System) and migrating some in\-house built invoice applications to the strategic platform. * Stakeholder Alignment: Collaborate with stakeholders across Finance, Tax, Operations, Regulatory Reporting to understand requirements, prioritise needs, and optimise invoicing solutions * Vendor Management: Manage vendor relationships, contracts and escalations effectively * Establish standardised operating models, governance controls, and quality frameworks across EMEA entities for invoice processing. * Monitor platform performance, risk, compliance, and audit findings, and leading the implementation of continuous improvements, remediation actions, and control uplifts **Required Experience** ----------------------- * Domain Knowledge: Good understanding of Order\-to\-cash (O2C) processes, including experience designing integrations between SaaS platforms and ERP / Finance systems (Accounts payable, Accounts Receivable) and billing. Ideally hands\-on experience with e\-invoicing SaaS platforms (e.g. PAGERO, COUPA etc.) * Experience operating as a product owner in technology or a change lead accountable for delivery, funded roadmaps and long\-term technology assets. * Agile Experience: Proven track record of working in agile/scrum methodologies, managing sprints and evaluating development progress with developers, QA and architects. * Delivery Management: Confident delivering at pace, across BAU, change delivery, testing cycles, and incident management * Strong communication, analytical and decision‑making skills, with a focus on structured problem‑solving and control improvement **Desirable Experience \& Education and Qualifications** -------------------------------------------------------- * Experience working in a hybrid business/technology domain * Degree‑level education in Information Technology, Computer Science, Finance, Business Information Systems or a related field (or equivalent professional experience) * Professional certifications such as ITIL, Agile/Scrum, or data management qualifications are advantageous but not essential **Competencies** ---------------- Customer Focus Driving Change Driving Results Embraces Diversity Enterprise Leadership Judgement and Decision Making Risk Management Strategic and Visionary Trust and Integrity**What we offer** ----------------- For all the expertise and experience you bring to help us make a difference, it’s only sustainable if we support you to build your career and be your best self so we offer competitive company benefits, centred around promoting positive well\-bring and work\-life balance. We also believe in fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, where all team members perspectives and contributions are valued. Initiatives in place which promote a diverse and inclusive culture and healthy work life balance include hybrid working, Sport \& Social Clubs and Diversity and Inclusion networks. As an employee of SMBC you have access to a host of both useful and exciting benefit offerings. See below some of the benefits on offer to you: * Hybrid and flexible working * Competitive paid leave days * Benefits to support your physical wellbeing, including private medical insurance and life and invalidity insurance * Various policies to support your mental wellbeing, including a robust behavioural health network with counselling and coaching services * Access a wide range of learning and development opportunities and career progression opportunities * Ambitious remuneration package So, if you like a challenge and want to continuously grow and develop in a role where you will be supported along the way by a dynamic and diverse team, apply today!! **About SMBC** -------------- A trusted partner for the long term United by a sense of purpose towards our customers \- to be a trusted partner for the long\-term \- and our universal banking platform in EMEA, we deliver a full suite of corporate finance products and solutions to our customers, including corporate, structured and trade finance, leveraged finance, loan market and treasury products. We can also provide investment banking and advisory services and a range of innovative solutions in global capital markets. about what we do and who we are at https://www.smbcgroup.com/emea/careers

This is a 6 month contract. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver on\-site and remote IT support during the go\-live phase. * Monitor, maintain, and troubleshoot Windows environments, including Active Directory and Azure AD. * Manage device provisioning, patching, and software deployment using SCCM, Intune, and Windows Autopatch. * Serve as the primary point of contact for incidents and service requests, ensuring prompt resolution with minimal business impact. * Maintain up\-to\-date documentation for systems, configurations, and incident resolutions. * Work closely with internal teams and external vendors to resolve technical issues. * Support business continuity and disaster recovery processes. * Automate routine tasks using PowerShell scripting. **Skills \& Experience** * Strong hands\-on experience with Microsoft Windows, Active Directory, and Azure AD. * Solid expertise in endpoint management and patching tools, including SCCM and Intune. * Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Experience with PowerShell scripting. **Qualifications** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience in IT support or operations. * Degree or diploma in IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline. * Microsoft certifications are an advantage.

title: Infrastructure Analyst

The role requires strong technical expertise across Microsoft cloud technologies, systems administration, virtualization, networking, security, vendor management, and ICT project delivery. Qualifications \& Experience Essential Qualifications Candidates should ideally possess: * A Level 8 qualification, as recognised on the National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ), or equivalent, in: + Computer Science + Information Technology + or another relevant discipline. Candidates holding a relevant Level 7 or Level 6 qualification will also be considered. Successful candidates appointed with these qualifications will be required to complete a Level 8 qualification (supported by the organisation) within a three\-year timeframe. Essential Experience * Minimum of 2 years' experience in an Enterprise ICT environment working as a Systems Administrator with a strong focus on Microsoft cloud services. * Demonstrated experience in: + Solution design + Solution implementation + System maintenance + Cost and billing management * 3\-5 years' hands\-on experience with relevant technologies including: + Microsoft Azure + Microsoft 365 / Office 365 + Azure Identity and Access Management + Exchange Online + Microsoft Teams + Active Directory + DHCP and DNS + VMware + Commvault * Microsoft certifications in relevant Azure and Microsoft 365 technologies. Desirable Qualifications \& Experience * Experience with comparable cloud technologies such as AWS. * Networking, firewall, web filtering, and application filtering knowledge and certifications such as: + CCNA + CCNP * ITIL v3/2011 or ITIL v4 certification. * PRINCE2 Project Management certification. * Experience with: + IPS / IDS + PAM + IAM + SIEM + Syslog technologies * Strong Microsoft Windows Server administration skills including: + Windows firewalls + Security hardening * Mobile Device Management experience using technologies such as: + Microsoft Intune + AirWatch * Public sector procurement experience. * Experience with ICT ticketing systems and service management. * Experience working within large ICT teams including: + Service Delivery + Networking + Security + Applications + Research \& Innovation Knowledge \& Skills The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Strong Systems Administration and/or Networking Administration skills. * Vendor and Managed Service Provider management experience. * Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder engagement skills. * Strong customer service orientation with the ability to anticipate and respond to changing business needs. * Procurement and supplier management capabilities. * ICT project management experience including planning, delivery, and coordination of technical projects. * Knowledge of ITIL Service Management methodology. * Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams. * Fluent English communication skills, both written and verbal. Behavioural Competencies The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Effective decision\-making within a business context. * A structured, organised, and methodical approach to work. * Commitment to quality service delivery and continuous improvement. * Strong resource planning and organisational capability. * Positive and supportive leadership behaviours. * Understanding of organisational and operational business context. * Strong information management and documentation skills. * Commitment to teamwork and collaboration. * Flexibility and adaptability in response to changing priorities. * Initiative and proactive problem\-solving. * Resilience and ability to perform under pressure while managing competing priorities. Main Duties \& Responsibilities The successful candidate will: * Contribute as a member of the Infrastructure Team to the delivery and support of enterprise infrastructure services. * Manage and administer virtual server environments and cloud infrastructure platforms. * Support and maintain technologies including: + Windows and Linux servers + Active Directory + VMware + SAN technologies + Commvault + Veeam + File sharing platforms + Monitoring platforms + Microsoft 365 + Azure + Azure AD / Entra ID + Microsoft Teams + Dynamics 365 CRM + Dataverse + PowerApps + Network infrastructure including Juniper, Dell, and Cisco devices * Provide technical guidance and oversight to Managed Service Providers and external suppliers. * Participate in Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity planning and testing activities. * Lead and support security hardening initiatives across cloud and on\-premise systems. * Assist in the configuration and management of mobile devices through Microsoft Intune. * Support procurement activities including drafting, reviewing, and evaluating tenders for ICT services, software, and hardware. * Participate in supplier and contract management activities including risk management and performance reviews. * Track and manage ICT hardware and software assets throughout their lifecycle. * Lead planning, testing, and deployment of: + Security patches + Operating system upgrades + Software upgrades * Support rollout and maintenance of security and monitoring tools. * Manage incident resolution and participate in problem management processes. * Project manage infrastructure and cloud migration initiatives. * Contribute to infrastructure documentation and technical standards. * Research and evaluate emerging technologies and service models. * Maintain continuous professional development and participate in performance management processes. * Support organisational quality customer service commitments. * Comply fully with health, safety, and organisational policies. * Undertake additional duties as assigned. Key Technologies * Microsoft Azure * Microsoft 365 / Office 365 * Azure Entra ID * Exchange Online * Microsoft Teams * Active Directory * VMware * Commvault * Veeam * Windows Server * Linux * DHCP / DNS * Intune * Cisco / Juniper / Dell networking * Dataverse * Power Platform * Dynamics 365 Desirable Certifications * Microsoft Azure Certifications * Microsoft 365 Certifications * CCNA / CCNP * ITIL v4 * PRINCE2 * Security\-related certifications Working Relationships The role involves close collaboration with: * ICT Infrastructure Teams * Service Delivery Teams * Security Teams * Managed Service Providers * Software and Hardware Vendors * Internal Business Stakeholders

The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

title: Internship Opportunities 2026
description: **At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.** Cadence is a pivotal leader in electronic design, building upon more than 30 years of computational software expertise. The company applies its underlying Intelligent System Design strategy to deliver software, hardware and IP that turn design concepts into reality. Cadence also offer everything else you would expect from a world\-class technology organization and a Fortune 100 company, which is ranked as one of the best places to work worldwide: https://fortune.com/best\-companies/2020/cadence Cadence Cork are recruiting key talent across several Universities to join our Internship Programme for 2026\. If you are passionate about technology and would love to learn and grow in a cross\-geographic and multi\-cultural organization, developing next\-generation software, then Cadence Cork is for you. As part of your experience with Cadence, you will join a new class of professionals in our Career Catalyst Program. Through this program, you’ll receive special opportunities to develop your leadership capabilities, learn from Cadence executives and other industry leaders, and socialize and network with your peers. ***At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.*** **Job Title: Intern Opportunities 2026 (Software / Hardware Design)** **Location: Cork/Galway/Dublin** **Job Overview** We aim to make you an integral part of the team by providing training, mentorship, and encouragement of your creative talents. Our goal is to equip students with practical work experience. We understand that it is best to learn by doing. We will provide tasks that enable you to learn while making meaningful technical contributions. As part of the Cadence Internship program, you will get the opportunity to support different Business Units across the following functions including; * Digital Design Signoff * Customer IC / Analog / RF Design * System Analysis – including Thermal Design Management, EM solvers ad CFD Analysis * PCB Design and Analysis * System Design and Verification **The wealth of knowledge, experience, learning and autonomous culture of the team makes it a great team to work with.** **Job Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as BS or MS or Phd student majoring in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science / Electrical / Electronic Engineering / Digital Humanities \& Information Technology or similar. * Looking for strong C\+\+, algorithms, and software development / scripting skills. * Understanding of analog/RF/custom IC design and verification practices is a strong plus. * Exposure to Cadence Virtuoso Layout and physical verification tools is a plus. * Team player, driven, self\-motivated and autonomous. * Excellent communication, presentation and customer service skills. * Problem Solving skills. **Additional Information** Cadence is committed to equal employment opportunity and employment equity throughout all levels of the organization. We strive to attract a qualified and diverse candidate pool and encourage diversity and inclusion in the workplace. **We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.**

title: JD Young Financial Operations Internship
description: **Who We Are** At JD.com, also known as JINGDONG, everything begins with a bold idea and the drive to make life smarter, faster, and more connected. What started in China has grown into one of the world’s most trusted technology and supply chain ecosystems, serving over 700 million customers. From retail and logistics to tech, property, and beyond, we’re all about innovation that actually makes a difference. **Our Global Reach** JD.com is expanding worldwide through a digitally intelligent, cross\-border supply chain and world\-class logistics network. With platforms like Joybuy connecting trusted products to customers everywhere, our teams stretch across China, the U.S., Europe, APAC, the Middle East, and beyond. Every year, we welcome over 20,000 students and graduates into our business, showing our commitment to young talent and the fresh ideas that drive our future. Join us and contribute to global projects from day one. Learn more about who we are and what we do: https://corporate.jd.com/ **What We Offer** We believe great ideas can come from anywhere, and often, they come from you. As an intern or graduate at JD.com, you’ll work on meaningful, hands\-on projects, learn from talented colleagues across our global network, and grow in a supportive, fast\-paced environment designed for development. With competitive compensation, great office locations, and a people\-first culture that champions continuous learning, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and kick\-start your career. **About Our Program** You’ll be joining the JD Vanguard (JDV) Graduate Program, our frontline leadership pathway for the next generation of supply chain talent. Over the course of a year, you’ll take on real projects, build technical and people\-leadership skills, and learn how JD Logistics operates at a global level, with the opportunity to extend and continue your journey. * Bill Reconciliation and Exception Handling: Review logistics costs, insurance premiums, and other bills, identify data discrepancies, and drive resolution of exceptions to ensure timely collection of receivables, reduce bad debt risk, and improve settlement efficiency and customer satisfaction. * Process Optimization and System Improvement: Analyze pain points in the settlement process, propose system function optimization requirements and drive their implementation, design automation tools to reduce manual intervention, enhance settlement efficiency and accuracy, and support business scalability. * Risk Control and Compliance Management: Monitor contract terms, settlement documents, and fund flows, identify compliance risks, and develop preventive measures to ensure compliance with financial policies and regulatory requirements, safeguarding company funds and maintaining a compliant corporate image. * Cross\-Department Collaboration and Support: Coordinate with business and technical departments to allocate resources, resolve bottlenecks in the settlement process, provide financial training and data support, promote policy implementation, and improve cross\-team collaboration efficiency and business health. * Data Analysis and Decision Support: Regularly produce settlement analysis reports, use data modeling to identify operational risks and optimization opportunities, provide decision\-making support to management, and assist in business strategy adjustments and goal achievement. * Accounts Receivable Management and Bad Debt Monitoring: Track accounts receivable aging and payment progress, develop and implement collection strategies, conduct statistical analysis of bad debt causes, and propose process improvement suggestions to ensure efficient cash flow and financial stability. **About you** * Currently pursuing a degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Supply Chain, or a related field * Strong analytical skills and comfort working with numbers and data * Interest in financial operations, billing, and process optimization * Basic understanding of Procure\-to\-Pay (P2P) and/or Order\-to\-Cash (O2C) processes is a plus * Proficiency in Excel and Microsoft Office tools * Detail\-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams * Problem\-solving mindset with the ability to identify process improvement opportunities * English proficiency is required * 6\-month full\-time internship, fully onsite in our Dublin office * ASAP start date preferred, with potential opportunity to convert to a full\-time position based on performance *At JD.com, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where every voice counts and every talent belongs. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any legally protected characteristic.* **Ready To Apply?** Don't leave yourself wondering 'what if'. Hit apply and see where your curiosity can take you at JD.com

title: Junior Account Executive

Now expanding to their eighth office in Dublin, they seek innovative minds to continue their impactful legacy. Account Executives (AEs) are the foundations of account management teams. title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence (in our brand new office!) and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Linux Administrator
description: We are at the heart of Ireland’s energy transition \- connecting, operating and transforming the electricity grid to support a low carbon, renewable future. We are proud to be a certified Great Place to Work, recognised for our culture built on trust, collaboration and inclusion. At EirGrid you will find supportive teams, opportunities to grow and meaningful work. We also offer secure employment with a range of flexible and family friendly working policies to support your life and wellbeing. If you want to know more about what EirGrid has to offer you, visit If you are ready to make an impact and grow your career in a purpose\-led organisation, your journey starts here. **The Opportunity** The Linux and Database team is well established within the IT Infrastructure Department and is responsible for the organisation’s extensive Linux, Oracle, PostGres and Microsoft SQL Server solutions. A particular focus of this team is to ensure the reliability of the various platforms whilst driving performance and functional improvements and new delivery mechanisms including virtualisation and cloud services. The Linux Administrator will assist in the development and support of EirGrid’s Linux estate on Physical and Virtual machines which underpin the organisation’s corporate functions and mission\-critical power system operation and market functions. The focus of the role will be to support major programs of work to upgrade and implement in premise and cloud\-based systems. The successful applicant will contribute to the overall success of Eirgrid by working as part of a team of IT professionals, undertaking Linux administration duties and driving project delivery and solution optimisation. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the Linux \& Database Team Lead in delivering on project targets and operational KPIs * Install, configure, and maintain Linux operating systems Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), Oracle Linux environments. * Monitor system performance, troubleshoot issues, and ensure high availability and reliability of Linux\-based infrastructure * Configure network interfaces (NICs), Ethernet devices, and manage ILO for HP servers * Manage storage, including SAN, NAS, RAID, multipathing, zoning, and LVM * Diagnose and troubleshoot software, hardware, and network issues to identify root causes * Manage package installations, updates, and maintain software inventories * Configure, monitor, and manage backups and disaster recovery plans * Ensure system uptime, compliance, and handle user access management * Develop and maintain automation scripts (Bash, Python) for routine tasks and monitoring * Handle change management processes, system upgrades, and patch deployments * Implement and manage OS firewalls, security configurations, and vulnerability management * Configure and troubleshoot virtualization environments (e.g., VMware). * Automate system administrative tasks using scripting languages such as Bash or Python to streamline processes and improve efficiency. * Evaluate and recommend hardware and software upgrades to improve system performance and scalability. * Provide in\-depth troubleshooting and issue resolution for the Red Hat and Oracle Linux ecosystem. * Investigate root causes of issues and escalate to engineering or vendor support when needed. * Support the asset management of Linux systems including inventory maintenance, warranty management, vendor support and configuration backups * Oversee the development of internal procedures, processes and documentation * Promote high standards of platform design, operation and availability and ensure that support capabilities during and outside normal working hours are maintained * Liaise with the EirGrid’s Governance \& Planning team and business representatives to understand business needs and the wider context of developing and delivering IT solutions * Provide effective stakeholder management of both internal and external stakeholders including strategic vendors and service providers * Proficiency in capacity planning and disaster recovery planning * Provide training and mentoring to junior staff **About You** The successful candidate will have: * An IT degree or recognised qualification in a relevant technical discipline * 7\+ years’ experience in delivering and/or operating critical business solutions * Proficiency in Red Hat and Oracle Linux OS environments * Expertise in networking protocols (TCP/IP, DHCP, DNS, VPN) and firewall management * Strong skills in SAN, NAS, RAID configurations, multipathing, and LVM * In\-depth experience with Bash and Python scripting for automation * Hands\-on experience with VMware and other virtualization technologies * Familiarity with TCP/IP, VLANs, load balancing, and high availability configurations * Installation \& configuration of Operating system, ILO * CPU Utilization,
