Together, the Flutter UK\&I brands offer market\-leading entertainment to millions of customers every single week! Over the last couple of decades, they have all ridden the wave of digital betting with industry firsts like ‘cash\-out’, ‘Request a Bet’, betting exchanges, and free\-to\-play games like Soccer Saturday Super 6\. **Working Here** Do you want to work somewhere extraordinary? From the people you spend your days with to the ground\-breaking projects, no two days will be the same. With a philosophy of ‘Together We Are More’, our 7,000\+ colleagues come together to form an expert community across technology, product, commercial, data, infrastructure, marketing, and a myriad of subject areas. We listen without judgement, encourage \& support, and help build others up. Working at Flutter UK\&I means you can be yourself, work how and where suits you best, and let your personality shine! **Introducing the Team** Flutter UK\&I are industry\-leading in the field of horse racing and are responsible for a significant percentage of the market within the region. The team is based across two locations, Dublin and Leeds, with the teams working closely together in a high\-paced environment. We pride ourselves on being able to understand our customers and give them the best experience possible on a day\-to\-day basis. Racing is an always\-on sport, and to reflect the priorities in the Racing calendar, you will be expected to adapt accordingly.

title: Trainee Actuary
description: **Job Title:** Trainee Actuary **Location:** Dublin **Working Model:** Hybrid Abrivia are currently recruiting for an Trainee Actuary to join our client within the insurance sector. This is an excellent opportunity for a developing actuarial professional to gain broad exposure across reserving, regulatory reporting, financial reporting, and actuarial analysis within a growing and collaborative team environment. Working closely with qualified actuaries, the successful candidate will support a range of actuarial activities while continuing their professional qualification journey. This role would suit someone with a strong technical foundation who is looking to broaden their experience across reserving, Solvency II, IFRS 17, and wider actuarial projects. **Your Role** * Support the preparation and analysis of actuarial reserves and quarterly reserving exercises. * Assist with Solvency II reporting and regulatory submissions. * Contribute to IFRS 17 reporting processes and associated analysis. * Support audit requirements and liaise with internal and external stakeholders where required. * Analyse claims trends, loss ratios, and portfolio performance to support business decision\-making. * Assist with actuarial modelling, reporting improvements, and system enhancements. * Contribute to board reporting, management information, and wider actuarial projects. **Your Skills** * 2–3\+ years' experience within an actuarial function, ideally within General Insurance. * Life, Health, or other insurance actuarial backgrounds will also be considered. * Progressing through actuarial exams, with a clear commitment towards qualification. * Experience across some or all of reserving, Solvency II, IFRS 17 reporting, audit support, loss ratio analysis, or risk appetite monitoring. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data. * Experience using actuarial software packages, with ResQ experience highly desirable. * Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. **Should this position be of interest to you please call Jordan at 015313739\. Applications are in strict confidence.**

title: Training Co-Ordinator Role - The CPL Institute
description: **Title:** Training Co\-Ordinator \- Full\-Time **Location:** Swords, Co. Dublin **Working Arrangement:** Hybrid (3 days office / 2 days home) **Salary:** €30,000 \- €34,000, Depending on Experience **About the Role** We're looking for a Training Co\-Ordinator to join the team at CPL Institute and help keep our nationwide operation running smoothly. This is a busy, varied role at the heart of what we do. If you're someone who likes being organised, enjoys working with people, and takes pride in getting the details right, you'll fit in well here. You'll be the operational backbone behind how our training programmes are planned, delivered, and recorded, working across a wide range of programmes from health and safety to healthcare and professional development. **What You'll Be Doing** No two days are exactly the same, but on any given week you can expect to be: * Demonstrating the ability to multitask effectively, manage multiple priorities simultaneously, and use your initiative when making decisions. * Managing bookings and scheduling \- confirming courses, coordinating instructors, and making sure everything is in place before training day * Being the first point of contact for clients \- answering queries, handling course changes, and making sure people have a positive experience from start to finish * Preparing and issuing training materials, processing results, and issuing certificates * Submitting results to organisations such as PHECC, QQI, IOSH and others as required * Maintaining accurate records across our internal systems * Supporting quality and compliance work, including internal verification, ISO management review meetings, and QQI Academic Council administration * Taking minutes and tracking actions from meetings * Contributing to the ongoing development of our processes and procedures **What We're Looking For** You don't need to tick every box, but the people who tend to thrive in this role usually have: * Experience working in a busy office or customer\-facing environment where accuracy and professionalism matter * Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple things at once without dropping the ball * Confidence using Microsoft Office, Word, Excel, Outlook in particular * Good communication skills, whether that's on the phone, over email, or in person * A More than anything, we're looking for someone who is reliable, positive, and easy to work with. You'll be part of a team that genuinely supports each other, and we want someone who brings that same energy. You'll need to be comfortable working independently when required, managing your own time, and flagging any issues early rather than letting them build up. **Working Arrangements** This is a hybrid role based at our Swords, Co. Dublin office. The standard working arrangement consists of three days in the office and two days working from home. However, from time to time, you may be required to attend the office for an additional day to support operational requirements and business needs.

title: Treasury Analyst (temporary 9 months)
description: Powering the Future of Biotech Finance Are you ready to play a key role in managing financial strategies for an innovative, fast\-growing biotech company? As a Treasury Analyst at Legend Biotech, you'll have a unique opportunity to work at the heart of our global Treasury operations in Dublin, Ireland. Your work will directly support cash management, forecasting, and reporting, all while contributing to our mission of revolutionizing cancer treatment. What can you expect? * Financial Strategy \& Cash Management: Prepare cash forecasts, intercompany funding, and FX projections, helping optimize liquidity and manage foreign exchange risks across the business. Your work will play a crucial part in the financial health of the company. * Reporting \& Executive Support: Produce detailed management reports on cash performance, cash management targets, and long\-term planning. Work closely with Treasury leadership to provide insights and prepare executive\-level presentations for key stakeholders. * Operational Efficiency: Support day\-to\-day Treasury operations, including opening/closing bank accounts, administering online banking systems, and managing corporate card and travel accounts. Your organizational skills will help maintain smooth financial operations. * Compliance \& Process Improvement: Ensure all processes align with regulatory standards and participate in audit reviews and process walkthroughs. You’ll also contribute to continuous improvements in Treasury processes, optimizing efficiency and enhancing controls. * Collaboration \& Innovation: Work with cross\-functional teams and be involved in projects that support both short\-term and long\-term business goals. Your teamwork will help drive operational excellence and innovation in the finance space. Who are we looking for? **Education** * Bachelor’s in Accounting or Finance **Experience** * A minimum of 3 years of progressive Treasury experience * Experience in a Global environment **Expertise** * Professional certifications such as ACT/IACT, CPA/CMA/ACCA/CIMA are a plus * Knowledge of SOX financial controls on Accounting and reporting processes is preferred * Experience with ERP systems (SAP preferred) **Language** * Fluent in English with excellent written and verbal communication skill **Strengths** * Collaborative and proactive, with the ability to manage multiple priorities * Strong communication skills to work with all levels of the organization * Excellent at financial modelling, forecasting, and reporting * Highly organized with a keen eye for detail and process improvement * Proficient in Excel, PowerPoint, and business reporting tools (Onestream preferred) **What do we offer you?** A fixed term contract of 9 months duration with a competitive salary package complemented by a range of fringe benefits. Work in a supportive and innovative environment at our Dublin office, where we encourage learning and personal development. Join a fast\-growing, global company with a unique blend of start\-up culture and financial stability, combining the cutting\-edge biotech space with the financial assurance of a Nasdaq\-listed company. **About Legend Biotech** Legend Biotech is a global leader in biotechnology, focused on developing, manufacturing, and commercializing life\-saving cell therapies to treat chronic and life\-threatening diseases like cancer. Founded in 2014, we are headquartered in Somerset, New Jersey, with over 2,400 employees across six global manufacturing sites. We’re committed to making a real difference in patients' lives worldwide. Legend Biotech is a proud equal opportunity/affirmative action employer committed to attracting, retaining, and maximizing the performance of a diverse and inclusive workforce. It is Legend’s policy to ensure equal employment opportunity without discrimination or harassment based on race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, national origin, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, genetic information, citizenship status, uniformed service member or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Legend Biotech maintains a drug\-free workplace. For information related to our privacy policy, please review: Legend Biotech Privacy Policy.

title: UX Designer
description: **Summary** You'll be the designer for our new product modules. You take the MVP scope \- which our PM defines from customer discovery \- and turn it into user flows, wireframes and clickable prototypes that engineers can build from, fast. You work within our existing Cloda design system (you extend it, you don't replace it), and you design for the full picture: happy paths, error and empty states, permissions, mobile and desktop. This is a hands\-on, embedded role. You'll report to the CTO and work most closely with our PM (who owns scope and customer discovery), our principal engineers, and the wider engineering team. You won't hand off and disappear \- you'll iterate with engineers as they build, and with customers as they react, refining the product based on real feedback rather than guesswork. Engineers start building in July, so they need build\-ready screens within your first couple of weeks. Speed matters from day one. Our PM is new, so we need someone self\-sufficient enough to read a spec, ask smart questions, and get moving without a lot of hand\-holding. **What We Need** * You've designed SaaS product modules from a spec before. Give you acceptance criteria and you produce MVP mockups and flows engineers can build from \- without needing weeks of discovery first. The PM does discovery; you design from the spec and ask smart questions. * You're fast \- in Figma and in prototyping. You produce usable wireframes in days, and you get to clickable prototypes quickly, using AI tools (Claude Design, Claude Code, v0, Codex, Figma's AI features) to move faster rather than polishing everything by hand. * You use AI to move faster, by default. We expect you to use AI tools to get from idea to clickable faster and deliver more value for customers. If AI isn't already part of how you work, this isn't the right role. * You think in flows and states, not just screens. Happy path, error, empty, permissions, mobile vs desktop, the messy edge cases \- and you've handled genuinely complex, business\-logic\-heavy flows, the kind a compliance product is full of. You're not a pure visual designer who just makes things pretty. * You've done this in a startup or fast\-moving product team. You've shipped real product fast, embedded with engineers, without heavy process, and owned real scope end to end \- not a narrow slice of someone else's product in a big, slow org. You've proven you can move \- that matters to us more than a big\-company logo. * You work well inside an existing design system. You extend patterns rather than replace them, you don't get precious about your designs, and you take direct feedback without friction. * You can design with incomplete information. Scope may still be moving when you start. You make sensible calls, flag your assumptions, and iterate \- rather than waiting for perfect certainty before you put anything on a screen. **Nice to Have** * Healthcare, clinical, or regulated\-industry understanding \- especially designing simple, clear interfaces for lower\-tech users on mobile / desktop. * Experience designing permission\-based, multi\-tenant products. * Experience with incident reporting, risk, or quality\-management products specifically. * A feel for AI\-native product thinking \- how chat, voice and "do it for me" actions change a workflow. .

title: UX Researcher
description: **We are looking for our future UX Researcher to join our Product teams in Dublin and our Global Customer Experience (CX) team!** **The role is offered as hybrid with 2 to 3 days per week onsite, in our Sandyford office.** As a UX Researcher on the Experience Insights (EXI) team (which is part of the larger Customer Experience team), you will work closely with designers, product managers and other stakeholders to create and refine digital experiences that improve the lives of our users. Using mixed\-methods research, you’ll support teams by bringing clarity to ambiguity with user insights throughout the product development process, from early\-stage discovery to launch and evaluation. The role is essential in delivering seamless and intuitive user experiences that are grounded in user needs. **What the role entails :** *Product Team Influence* * Develop research plans with clear objectives, participant screeners, moderator guides, recruiting strategies, and timelines * Share research findings with product, design, and executive teams in ways that support decisions * Provide regular project updates, including status, blockers, and open questions * Recommend appropriate research methods and tools based on stakeholder goals * Work with Product Managers to forecast and prioritize research needs *Organizational Influence:* * Join cross\-functional initiatives, representing the user voice and helping to drive continuous improvement * Build user empathy across the organization through readouts and direct feedback *UXR Influence:* * Stay current on industry trends and best practices in UX research **What You Bring To The Role** *Empathy and User Advocacy :* * You center users in your work. You understand their experiences, emotions, and motivations, and you make sure others understand them too. You use interviews, surveys, and usability tests to uncover what matters most. *Analytical Thinking :* * You are confident in both qualitative and quantitative methods and know when to use each. You turn findings into clear, actionable insights that guide decisions. *Communication and Collaboration* * You present insights in a clear and engaging way. You work closely with cross\-functional teams to make sure research findings lead to meaningful improvements. *Technical Proficiency* * You know how to select and use research tools to manage studies, analyze data, and communicate results. You apply these tools effectively in your day\-to\-day work. *Creativity* * You try new approaches when needed and adapt your communication for different audiences. You help your team explore fresh ways to apply research in their work. **Qualifications and Experience :** *Required:* * 3\-5 years of UX research experience, ideally in health tech or regulated industries * Comfortable conducting research in a fast\-paced, cross\-functional environment * Portfolio that shows past research work and deliverables * Experience with tools such as UserZoom, UserTesting, Qualtrics, Confluence, and Miro or Whiteboard * Strong communication and collaboration skills * Comfortable using AI tools as part of the research workflow * Degree in Human\-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Anthropology, or a related field * Willingness and ability to travel internationally *Preferred:* * Experience working in healthcare or medical devices * Experience conducting research on hardware/physical products **Additional information :** * At Resmed, all employees benefit from a bonus plan, the percentage of which depends on your position within the organisation. * Competitive benefits * Working from home flexibility * You also have access to a referral bonus and to Resmed's preferred shareholding programme * Internal career opportunity \- joining an international fast\-pace and massively growing company **OK, so what's next?** Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! We commit to respond to every applicant.
